title: Tecoma aurea
taxonomy: Tecoma aurea is a flowering plant that belongs to the family Bignoniaceae. This family is a group of flowering plants that are native to the tropical and subtropical regions of the world. The Bignoniaceae family, also known as the trumpet vine family, comprises over 800 species of plants, many of which are ornamental. Tecoma aurea, commonly known as the yellow trumpet flower, is a popular ornamental plant due to its bright yellow flowers and ability to attract hummingbirds and other pollinators. Its classification as a member of the Bignoniaceae family highlights its unique characteristics and adaptations that have allowed it to thrive in its native habitats.
uses: Tecoma aurea is also known as the yellow trumpetbush or trumpet tree.  Tecoma aurea, also known as Yellow Trumpetbush, has been used in traditional medicine in various parts of the world. The plant is said to have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are used to treat various health issues. It has been used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory problems, as well as skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Additionally, the plant's leaves and flowers are used to make tea, which is believed to have various health benefits.
benefits: Tecoma aurea, also known as the Yellow Trumpet Creeper, has been traditionally used in folk medicine in various parts of the world. parts: Tecoma aurea, also known as Yellow Trumpetbush, is a plant with several medicinal properties. preparations: Tecoma aurea, a plant native to South America, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Herbal preparations made from Tecoma aurea are often used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. side_effects: Tecoma aurea, also known as the Yellow Trumpet Bush, is not typically associated with severe side effects.
